assumptions: - The outputs A_greater, A_equal, and A_less are determined based on the comparison of A and B.
- A_greater is set to 1 if A > B, A_equal is set to 1 if A == B, and A_less is set to 1 if A < B.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
All output combinations correspond to the comparator truth table
cases.goal: Validate combinational mapping across a representative set of inputs
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Ensures mutual exclusivity of A_greater, A_equal, and A_less
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known non-control inputs: ['A', 'B']
Drive each possible A,B pair in a single test vector
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe outputs after input stabilization (e.g., 1 ns delay)

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
Outputs are correct for minimum and maximum operand values
cases.goal: Validate edge behavior of the comparator
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Covers min (0) and max (7) values for both operands
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['A', 'B']
Apply A=0,B=7 and A=7,B=0 along with equal extremes
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.

expected_properties: All 64 input combinations produce exactly one high output among A_greater, A_equal, A_less
cases.goal: Full combinational truth table verification for the 3-bit comparator
notes: Runs through every possible A and B value sequentially
stimulus_intent: Iterate A and B over the range 0 to 7
timing_assumptions: Sample outputs after each input change stabilizes

expected_properties: Outputs are correct for the minimum and maximum operand values
cases.goal: Validate extreme boundary conditions of the comparator
notes: Test cases include A=0,B=0; A=7,B=7; A=0,B=7; A=7,B=0

expected_properties: Outputs track input changes without glitches or metastability
cases.goal: Check output stability under rapid input toggling
notes: Toggle A and B through a sequence of values to stress combinational paths
stimulus_intent: Apply a fast-changing sequence of A and B values
timing_assumptions: Immediate observation after each input change
